people.name: Daniel Jones
people.title: Managing Partner
people.persona: Daniel is the firm's final signature and knows it; nothing reaches a client until he has read it end to end and can stand behind every line. He thinks in terms of what the client's own executive will take away from a single skim, and he trims anything that does not survive that test. His voice is plain, confident, and unhurried, the senior partner who would rather say one useful thing clearly than three that hedge.

people.name: Robert Miller
people.title: Principal Consultant
people.persona: Robert carries engagements from kickoff through delivery and treats the client relationship as his to protect. He is most at home in an operational review, walking a floor and turning what he sees into a short list of things worth fixing. His writing is direct and practical, built around recommendations a busy operator can act on, and he would rather be concrete than impressive.

people.name: Jennifer Fletcher
people.title: Principal Consultant
people.persona: Jennifer leads engagements with a structured, methodical hand and is the partner most likely to insist the numbers reconcile before anyone briefs the client. She gravitates toward pricing studies and the discipline of integration work, where sequence and follow-through matter. Her tone is measured and organized; she runs a tight meeting and writes minutes that leave no attendee guessing what they agreed to.

people.name: Susan Wiley
people.title: Analyst
people.persona: Susan does the underlying analysis the principals build on, turning scattered inputs into clean exhibits and first drafts. She is careful and quietly thorough, the kind of analyst who flags the inconsistency everyone else skimmed past. When she writes it is tidy and understated, usually the raw material a principal will later shape for the client's eyes.

people.name: Michelle Lopez
people.title: Office Manager
people.persona: Michelle keeps the firm running so the consultants can stay in front of clients, handling the letterhead, the scheduling, and the small logistics that hold an engagement together. She is organized, personable, and the person who knows where everything is and when everything is due. Her writing is friendly and efficient, the practical note that confirms a time or chases a missing piece without any fuss.

people.name: Cynthia Ball
people.title: Analyst
people.persona: Cynthia is the analyst who likes a hard question and a spreadsheet to answer it, most in her element pulling apart a pricing question until the drivers are clear. She is sharp and a little skeptical, quick to ask whether an assumption actually holds. Her drafts are crisp and evidence-first, laid out so a principal can see how she got there and lift what they need.

timeline.summary: Firm moved into its current office.
